reasons: Eudora can burst a target that Akai pins or displaces into a fixed position.
Akai's control gives Eudora a clearer window to land her single-target magic combo.

proof.summary: Akai isolates or pins a target with Heavy Spin, and Eudora follows with magic burst during the control window.
worksBestWhen: Akai pins a squishy target near terrain.
Eudora is already in combo range.
The team focuses the same target after Akai commits.
failureCases: Akai pushes the target out of Eudora's range.
Eudora is too far to follow the pin.
The target receives peel, shield, or magic-defense support.

reasons: Saber can burst a target that Akai separates from the enemy team.
Akai's displacement helps create the isolation that Saber wants for Triple Sweep.

proof.summary: Akai separates or pins a priority enemy, and Saber follows with single-target airborne burst.
worksBestWhen: Akai isolates a fragile backline target.
Akai pins away from enemy peel.
failureCases: Akai displaces the target outside Saber's reach.
Saber commits to a durable target.
Enemy teammates collapse on Saber after entry.

reasons: Pharsa can follow Akai's displacement and zoning with long-range AoE damage.
Akai's frontline pressure helps Pharsa cast from a safer position.

proof.summary: Akai pins or zones enemies, and Pharsa follows with ranged damage into the controlled area.
worksBestWhen: Akai controls enemies near terrain or an objective.
Pharsa has safe vision and range to cast.
Akai does not push enemies out of Pharsa's damage area.
failureCases: Enemy assassins reach Pharsa before follow-up.
Enemies disengage from the controlled zone.

reasons: Vale can layer knock-up, slow, and burst after Akai pins or disrupts enemies.
Akai's control helps Vale connect AoE skills on targets that have limited movement.

proof.summary: Akai controls enemy movement with displacement, and Vale follows with crowd control plus magic burst into the same target area.
worksBestWhen: Akai pins or zones a priority target.
Vale holds cooldowns for the follow-up window.
Both heroes focus the same enemy group or carry.
failureCases: Vale casts before Akai finishes positioning the target.
Enemies spread before Vale's AoE lands.
The target escapes the control chain with mobility or protection.

reasons: Claude can enter after Akai disrupts the enemy formation and creates space.
Akai can pin or zone enemies so Claude has a clearer Blazing Duet damage window.

proof.summary: Akai starts the displacement and zoning, while Claude follows with Blazing Duet after the enemy formation is disrupted.
worksBestWhen: Akai controls the enemy frontline or pins a key target.
Claude waits until Akai creates space.
The team is ready to protect Claude during his entry.
failureCases: Akai pushes enemies out of Claude's damage area.
Claude enters before Akai controls enemy peel.
Enemy displacement or burst forces Claude out of the fight.
